commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a reply-operation after-clear helper, routes dormant follow-up drain scheduling through it, and adds E2E coverage for immediate versus deferred drain startup.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +31. Total +48 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still disables enqueue-owned idle restart for active-run follow-ups and the linked issue contains Slack and WhatsApp queued_work_without_active_run evidence. I did not run a live timing reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair job is needed because there is no discrete patch defect; maintainers need to choose the canonical fix and handle protected-label merge review.

target.title: fix(queue): restart dormant followup drains
target.body:
## Summary

- Restart a dormant follow-up drain when enqueue races with completion of the active reply run.
- Register the drain against the reply-lane owner so collect-mode batching and queued typing remain intact until lane release.
- Consolidate session-id adoption for both normal run completion and the race-recovery path.

Reimplements the useful idea from #92284 at the reply-run ownership boundary. Thanks @xzh-xydt for the original investigation and test case.

Fixes #91909.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue.collect.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue.drain-restart.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts` (152 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- fresh autoreview: clean
- Crabbox Azure `run_40cf3f5e6c81` / `cbx_d97ad8f9ba04`: `pnpm check:changed` passed for core and core-tests lanes (typecheck, lint, guards, import cycles)

Behavior addressed: queued follow-up messages no longer remain dormant when enqueue occurs after the active run has already released its queue-drain handoff.

Real environment tested: normal OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the repository Node/pnpm toolchain.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused auto-reply, E2E, collect-queue, drain-restart, and reply-run-registry test files listed above.

Evidence after fix: the new E2E cases prove immediate drain startup when no reply owner remains and deferred startup when an owner still holds the lane; all 152 focused tests pass.

Observed result after fix: the drain starts exactly after reply-lane release, without starting an admission retry while the original run is active.

What was not tested: live channel ingress; the affected queue and reply-lane lifecycle is channel-independent and is covered deterministically at the run/queue boundary.
